title: Tool Approval Flow
description: Human-in-the-loop approval gating: request approval, await decision, approve or reject tool call, handle interruptions
activity_flow: START: Model emits tool call (ResponseFunctionToolCall)
_FunctionToolBatchExecutor.execute() → creates async task per tool via _create_tool_task()
_run_single_tool() → calls _maybe_execute_tool_approval(func_tool, tool_call)
_maybe_execute_tool_approval() → calls function_needs_approval(func_tool, ctx, tool_call)
function_needs_approval() → evaluates func_tool.needs_approval (bool OR async callable(ctx, params, call_id))
BRANCH: needs_approval == False → return None (skip approval, proceed to execute tool body)
BRANCH: needs_approval == True → check context_wrapper.get_approval_status(tool_name, call_id)
BRANCH approval_status == True (already approved) → return None (proceed to execute)
BRANCH approval_status == False (already rejected) → resolve_approval_rejection_message() → return FunctionToolResult with rejection
BRANCH approval_status == None (no decision yet) → create ToolApprovalItem → return FunctionToolResult(run_item=approval_item) — INTERRUPT
_build_function_tool_results() → collects results; ToolApprovalItems become interruptions
RunResult.interruptions populated with ToolApprovalItem list — run PAUSES
Caller: result.to_state() → RunState serialized (supports to_string()/to_json())
User iterates result.interruptions → inspects ToolApprovalItem (agent.name, tool_name, arguments)
BRANCH: User approves → state.approve(interruption, always_approve=False|True)
BRANCH: User rejects → state.reject(interruption, always_reject=False|True, rejection_message=None|str)
Caller: Runner.run(agent, state) → run RESUMES
_maybe_execute_tool_approval() re-evaluates → approval_status now True or False
BRANCH approved → tool body executes normally → FunctionToolResult with output
BRANCH rejected → rejection message resolved → FunctionToolResult with rejection string
If new approval needed (nested or new tool call) → re-enter interruption loop
END: No more interruptions → RunResult.final_output returned
branching_conditions: needs_approval (bool | async callable) on FunctionTool / ShellTool / ApplyPatchTool / Agent.as_tool / HostedMCPTool
approval_status: None = no decision yet → interrupt; True = approved → proceed; False = rejected → reject message
always_approve=True: stores sticky approval for all future calls to that tool in this run
always_reject=True: stores sticky rejection for all future calls to that tool in this run
on_approval callback present (ShellTool / ApplyPatchTool / CustomTool): auto-approve/reject without surfacing interruption
Partial resolution: not all interruptions need to be resolved in one pass; unresolved ones re-surface
key_classes_and_methods: resolve_approval_status(tool_name, call_id, raw_item, agent, context_wrapper, ..., on_approval) -> tuple[bool|None, ToolApprovalItem]
execute_approved_tools(agent, interruptions, context_wrapper, generated_items, run_config, hooks)
data_objects: ToolApprovalItem: agent, raw_item, tool_name, tool_namespace, tool_origin, tool_lookup_key — surfaced in RunResult.interruptions
FunctionToolResult: tool, output, run_item (ToolApprovalItem when pending), interruptions (nested), agent_run_result
RunState: serializable; holds approval decisions, usage, trace metadata, nested agent-as-tool resumptions
nested_agent_approval: Agent.as_tool() can have its own needs_approval at the tool level (outer interruption) AND nested tools inside may raise their own approvals
_resolve_nested_tool_run_result(): peek stored result, if unresolved interruptions exist keep pending; else consume
Nested interruptions surface on the outer run; resume the original top-level agent, not the nested one
automatic_approval_path: resolve_approval_status() → if approval_status is None AND on_approval provided → call on_approval(ctx, approval_item)
on_approval returns {approve: bool, reason?: str} → context_wrapper.approve_tool() or reject_tool()
Re-query approval_status → run continues without surfacing interruption to caller
hosted_mcp_approval: process_hosted_mcp_approvals(): filters McpApprovalRequest items, checks context_wrapper approval status
If approved/rejected → emit MCPApprovalResponseItem with approve bool and optional reason
If no decision → append ToolApprovalItem to pending list → surfaces as interruption
serialization_durability: RunState.to_string() / to_json(): persist paused state to disk/DB; includes sticky approvals
RunState.from_string() / from_json(): restore state in any process, resume with Runner.run(agent, state)
context_serializer / context_deserializer / strict_context / context_override options for non-mapping contexts
important_notes: Approval is run-wide; same flow for current agent, handoff targets, and Agent.as_tool() nesting
Partial approval resolution allowed: unresolved interruptions re-surface on next Runner.run()
Custom rejection messages: per-call rejection_message on state.reject() overrides run-wide tool_error_formatter
Streaming: same flow via Runner.run_streamed(); drain stream_events() before inspecting interruptions
